Buy cheap goods (price <= Cheap) directly into warehouse inventory
every cycle, without needing a ship present at the port. Mirrors the
existing warehouse sell scan. Ships being in transit no longer blocks
opportunistic stockpiling at warehouse ports.

feat(assistant): assistant texte V1 déterministe — DEP-0361 à DEP-0400

Moteur texte complet, sans LLM, branché sur le panier via adapter mock.

## packages/types
- src/assistant.ts : types partagés (AssistantMessage, ParsedIntent,
  CatalogProduct, MatchResult, AssistantResponse, CartAdapter, CartItem)

## packages/utils
- assistant/normalize.ts  : normalisation (accents, casse, ponctuation) +
  extraction de mots-clés avec liste de mots vides français
- assistant/quantity.ts   : extraction de quantité (chiffres + nombres en
  lettres jusqu'à 10)
- assistant/parse-intent.ts : détection d'intention par règles
  (add / remove / replace / vague / unknown)
- assistant/catalog-match.ts : scoring et matching des produits par mots-clés
  (exact ≥2 pts, partiel 1 pt, top-3 candidats)
- assistant/replies.ts    : réponses courtes en français
- assistant/corrections.ts : logique remove (par panier), replace (prompt),
  raffinement de mots-clés pour requêtes composées
- __tests__/assistant.test.ts : 37 tests unitaires — tous passent ✅

## apps/web
- next.config.ts          : transpilePackages pour les packages workspace
- src/app/layout.tsx      : layout Next.js App Router
- src/app/page.tsx        : page d'accueil avec AssistantPanel
- components/assistant/assistant-panel.tsx  : panneau principal (état, scroll)
- components/assistant/assistant-message.tsx : bulle de message user/assistant
- components/assistant/assistant-input.tsx   : champ texte + bouton Envoyer
- lib/assistant-adapter.ts : pipeline processMessage() + MockCartAdapter V1
- lib/demo-catalog.ts     : 19 produits dépanneur (1 indisponible pour test)

## Exemples supportés
- "je veux du lait"        → ajout Lait 2% au panier
- "mets 2 coke"            → ajout 2× Coca-Cola
- "enlève le lait"         → retrait du panier
- "je veux des chips"      → clarification (3 marques proposées)
- "je sais pas quoi prendre" → message d'aide + suggestions
- "as-tu du pepsi zero"    → ajout Pepsi Zero
- "remplace par autre chose" → invite à préciser

commit.message:
Update to .NET 10 SDK with workload version set pinning (#3514)

Upgrade SkiaSharp from .NET 8 SDK to .NET 10 SDK with comprehensive build system,
CI pipeline, TFM architecture, and workload management changes.

TFM Architecture:
- Restructured from Previous/Current/Next to Base/Previous/Current
- TFMBase=net6.0 (class libraries), TFMPrevious=net9.0, TFMCurrent=net10.0
- Dropped TFMNext (platform TFMs only support 2 versions at once)
- Added Previous TPVs for all platforms alongside Current TPVs

SDK & Tooling:
- global.json: SDK 8.0.100 -> 10.0.100, rollForward latestMinor -> latestFeature
- latestFeature allows any 10.0.xxx feature band across heterogeneous CI agents
- .NET SDK pipeline variable: 8.0.304 -> 10.0.103
- Xcode: 15.4 -> 26.2 (managed), 15.4 -> 16.2 (native)
- Docker images: dotnet/sdk:8.0 -> dotnet/sdk:10.0
- Emscripten: 3.1.34 -> 3.1.56
- macOS agents: macos-14 -> macos-15
- Android tests moved from macOS to Linux agents

Workload Management:
- Replaced ad-hoc manifest mode with deterministic workload version set pinning
- Official workloads install with 'dotnet workload install --version 10.0.104'
- Tizen installs separately (not part of official workload sets)
- Removed DOTNET_VERSION_PREVIEW, DOTNET_WORKLOAD_SOURCE, installPreviewVs
- Removed manifest mode workarounds (--skip-manifest-update)

Platform Versions (Previous / Current):
- Android: 35.0 / 36.0
- iOS: 18.0 / 26.2
- Mac Catalyst: 18.0 / 26.2
- tvOS: 18.0 / 26.2
- macOS: 15.0 / 26.2
- Tizen: 8.0 / 10.0

Package Updates:
- xunit: 2.4.2 -> 2.9.3
- xharness CLI: 9.0.0-prerelease.24317.3 -> 11.0.0-prerelease.26107.1
- DeviceRunners: 0.1.0-preview.1 -> 0.1.0-preview.6
- TypeScript MSBuild: 5.7.1 -> 5.9.3
- Uno.Sdk: 5.2.175 -> 6.5.31

Blazor TypeScript Fix:
- Replaced TypeScriptCompileBlocked with per-TFM TypeScriptOutDir to fix
   multi-TFM parallel build race condition on shared wwwroot/*.js files

CI Cleanup:
- Removed old NuGet feeds (dotnet6/7/8, test-device-runners) from nuget.config
- Fixed Android emulator XDG path mismatch on Linux via ANDROID_AVD_HOME
- Added ANDROID_PLATFORM_VERSIONS: 21,35,36

Samples:
- MAUI: Removed Tizen platform folder, removed UseMauiCompatibility
- Uno: Updated to 6.5, migrated to UnoPlatformHostBuilder
- All: Updated TFMs and SupportedOSPlatformVersion minimums

Documentation:
- New: documentation/updating-dotnet-version.md for future upgrades

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: RBAC identity targets, private DNS zone validation, PE preconditions

Code review findings from automated validation pass:

1. AcrPull RBAC: changed from cluster control-plane identity to kubelet
   identity (properties.identityProfile.kubeletidentity.objectId).
   The kubelet identity is the one that pulls images, not the cluster
   SystemAssigned identity.

2. Key Vault Certificate User RBAC: changed from cluster identity to
   the App Routing add-on managed identity
   (properties.ingressProfile.webAppRouting.identity.objectId).
   App Routing fetches TLS certs using its own identity.

3. Added response_export_values for kubelet and App Routing identities
   so the objectIds are available for RBAC assignments.

4. Private DNS zone + SystemAssigned: clarified in variable description
   that custom zone IDs require UserAssigned identity (module uses
   SystemAssigned). Added handling for private_dns_zone_id='none' to
   automatically enable public FQDN.

5. ACR and Key Vault preconditions: both resources now validate that a
   PE subnet is available before creating private-access-only resources.
   Prevents unreachable services when external_pe_subnet_id is omitted
   in vending mode.

Validated: terraform init + validate + fmt -check all pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: ALZ Corp + vending + AVNM IPAM aligned module

Major restructure to align with ALZ Corp deployment model where the
subscription vending module pre-provisions spoke networking via AVNM
IPAM, and this module consumes pre-existing subnet IDs.

Terraform changes:
- dependencies.tf: new file with ACR (Premium, private endpoint, DNS
  zone group, AcrPull RBAC), Key Vault (RBAC auth, private endpoint,
  DNS zone group, Certificate User RBAC), Network Contributor on VNet,
  PE subnet. All azapi, no azurerm resources.
- locals.tf: three networking modes (vending external subnets, module-
  created VNet, AKS managed). PE subnet ID resolved from all paths.
- variables.tf: external_node/apiserver/pe_subnet_id inputs for
  vending mode. ACR + KV variables (names required, no defaults for
  globally unique names). Private DNS zone IDs for PE DNS registration.
  NAT Gateway removed. enable_byo_vnet=true (always BYO for Corp).
  egress_type defaults to userDefinedRouting (hub firewall).
- network.tf: NAT Gateway resources removed. Header updated to note
  file is skipped when external subnets are provided.
- outputs.tf: NAT Gateway output removed. ACR, KV, PE subnet outputs
  added.
- terraform.tfvars.example: rewritten for Corp vending (Scenario 1)
  and standalone (Scenario 2). Shows private DNS zone IDs from
  connectivity subscription.

Documentation:
- README: usage example, Cilium vs Enterprise comparison, day-2 ops
  (Karpenter CRDs, CI/CD private access, backup/DR, cost), workload
  identity setup, Cilium network policy, GitOps, certificate rotation
- copilot-instructions: updated file layout, removed NAT Gateway

Design decisions:
- enable_byo_vnet=true in both vending and standalone (BYO from AKS
  perspective in both cases)
- NAT Gateway removed entirely (Corp = hub firewall egress)
- Private DNS Zones NOT created by this module (owned by connectivity
  subscription). DNS Zone Groups auto-register PE A records.
- Network Contributor RBAC only created when this module creates the
  VNet (in vending mode, vending pipeline handles it)
- SystemAssigned identity with Terraform dependency ordering

Validated: terraform init + terraform validate pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: isolate sub-agent output from main chat and render in card

Sub-agent assistant text and reasoning were leaking into the main chat
transcript. Route sub-agent streaming deltas and final messages into
per-subagent accumulators keyed by tool call ID, using ParentToolCallId
from the SDK with state-based fallback. Persist transcript/reasoning in
the sub-agent payload JSON for rebuild, and update SubagentToolCallItem
directly via TranscriptBuilder for immediate UI visibility inside the
expandable sub-agent card.

Files changed:
- ChatViewModel.SessionLifecycle.cs: sub-agent stream routing and state
- ChatViewModel.cs: BuildSubagentPayloadJson transcript/reasoning fields
- TranscriptBuilder.cs: UpdateSubagentTranscript/ReasoningText methods
- TranscriptItems.cs: TranscriptText/ReasoningText properties on card
- TranscriptPaging.cs: weight estimation for sub-agent card content
- ChatView.axaml: render reasoning + transcript inside sub-agent card
- ChatView.axaml.cs: treat active sub-agent cards as streaming content

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Istio gateway: changed mode from External to Internal (Corp pattern)
- Image Cleaner: default interval corrected to 168h (1 week per official docs)
- NAT GW output: description corrected to 'Resource ID' not 'IP address'
- Identity: added comment noting UserAssigned required for custom privateDNSZone

README:
- App Routing: corrected to Ingress API (IngressClass: webapprouting.kubernetes.azure.com)
  not Gateway API. Added NGINX retirement notice (maintenance ended Mar 2026,
  Microsoft patches through Nov 2026, Gateway API migration planned)
- Istio: corrected to Istio Gateway CRD + VirtualService/DestinationRule,
  not Kubernetes Gateway API (not yet supported in AKS Istio add-on)
- AGC: retained with limitation callout, noted private IP expected but no ETA
- Egress FQDNs: acs-mirror.azurelinux.com -> acs-mirror.azureedge.net,
  added mcr-0001.mcr-msedge.net, noted *.hcp not required for private clusters
- RBAC: Private DNS Zone Contributor for private zones, DNS Zone Contributor
  for public zones, Key Vault Certificate User for App Routing TLS
- Image Cleaner: default corrected to 7 days (168h) throughout
- Custom CA trust: updated to reflect AKS >= v20260408 support on NAP,
  HTTP Proxy also now supported on NAP clusters
- Topology: clarified module implements 2 subnets, AGC/PE subnets documented
  as target architecture for when those services are deployed
- Mermaid diagram: removed AGC from Corp ingress path, corrected controller labels

- Same RBAC, FQDN, App Routing, and DNS corrections applied

commit.message:
fix(recall): add WITH clause between YIELD and WHERE for KuzuDB compatibility

KuzuDB's CALL...YIELD syntax does not support a WHERE clause directly
after YIELD. A WITH clause must be inserted between YIELD and WHERE.

Fixes scoped recall queries for both vector (QUERY_VECTOR_INDEX) and
full-text (QUERY_FTS_INDEX) search paths.

commit.message:
fix: AGC does not support private IP frontends - correct Corp ingress guidance

AGC frontends only expose public FQDNs. Private IP addresses are
not currently supported (no ETA from Microsoft). This makes AGC
unsuitable for ALZ Corp scenarios requiring fully private ingress.

- AGC section: removed 'recommended for Corp', added limitation
  callout with source reference to MS Learn
- App Routing add-on promoted to recommended Corp ingress (internal LB)
- Mermaid diagram: Corp ingress flow now routes through internal LB
  to App Routing, not AGC. AGC shown as public-only.
- Traffic flow table updated
- Ingress comparison table: AGC marked as not Corp-suitable
